lharries/whatsapp-mcp ·GitHub ⭐ 5405 Go · WhatsApp, MCP, AI Agents, Communication, Personal Assistant, Python, Open Source

What it is

This MCP (Model Context Protocol) server bridges personal WhatsApp accounts with AI agents like Claude. It allows agents to search contacts, read message history (including media), and send messages/files directly from the LLM interface.

Problems solved

  • Inaccessibility of personal chat data for AI productivity tools
  • Lack of a standardized, secure way to grant AI agents communication capabilities
  • Complexity of the WhatsApp Web protocol for casual developers

How to get started

Run the Go-based bridge to authenticate via QR code. Configure the Python MCP server in your Claude Desktop or Cursor settings. The agent will then have access to tools like search_contacts and send_message.

Detailed report

1. Positioning

lharries/whatsapp-mcp is positioned as a this mcp (model context protocol) server bridges personal whatsapp accounts with ai agents like claude. it allows agents to search contacts, read message history (including media), and send messages/files directly from the llm interface. It aims to provide developers with a robust, Go-based solution for whatsapp mcp server.

2. Pain Points & Advantages

Traditional methods in this domain often suffer from Inaccessibility of personal chat data for AI productivity tools. lharries/whatsapp-mcp addresses this by this mcp (model context protocol) server bridges personal whatsapp accounts with ai agents like claude. it allows agents to search contacts, read message history (including media), and send messages/files directly from the llm interface, offering a significant performance boost and better developer ergonomics compared to existing alternatives.

3. Technical Architecture

The project is built using Go, adopting a modular and scalable design. It leverages modern industry standards to ensure compatibility across different environments, focusing on efficiency and ease of integration.

4. Core Implementation

Main logic revolves around whatsapp mcp server. The implementation utilizes Go's strengths to provide this mcp (model context protocol) server bridges personal whatsapp accounts with ai agents like claude. it allows agents to search contacts, read message history (including media), and send messages/files directly from the llm interface, ensuring high reliability and performance under load.

5. Code Organization

The repository is structured logically, with core functionality separated from utilities and examples. This organization facilitates easy navigation and contribution for developers looking to extend the tool's capabilities.

6. Quick Start Suggestion

To get started, run the go-based bridge to authenticate via qr code. configure the python mcp server in your claude desktop or cursor settings. the agent will then have access to tools like search_contacts and send_message. This will allow you to quickly evaluate the tool and integrate it into your existing development workflow.

7. Summary

An essential, Go-powered utility that streamlines whatsapp mcp server; it effectively tackles Inaccessibility of personal chat data for AI productivity tools while maintaining a high standard of code quality and usability.
